ucs.title: [LLM] BRONZE BUTLER (Tick) DLL search-order hijacking via signed Japanese/AV binary in user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts the signature BRONZE BUTLER loader pattern: a legitimately-signed binary (Symantec/Trend Micro/JustSystems Ichitaro/Skype/Sysinternals) is dropped to a user-writable directory (AppData, Temp, ProgramData, Public) and side-loads an unsigned DLL — the documented delivery mechanism for Daserf, Datper and xxmm against Japanese targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("ichitaro.exe","Skype.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","MsMpEng.exe","ccSvcHst.exe","NisSrv.exe","PWMTR32V.DLL","vmtoolsd.exe","OUTLFLTR.DLL","NTWDBLIB.dll","TmDbgLog.exe","coreServiceShell.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval exe_dir=replace(process_path,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$","") | join type=inner dest exe_dir [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval exe_dir=replace(file_path,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$","") | rename file_name as sideloaded_dll, count as dll_writes] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspect_signers = dynamic(["Symantec Corporation","Trend Micro Inc.","Skype Technologies S.A.","JustSystems Corporation","Microsoft Corporation","Sysinternals - www.sysinternals.com","Google LLC"]);
let writable_paths = dynamic([@"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\appdata\roaming\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\perflogs\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName in~ (suspect_signers)
| extend exe_path_l = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where exe_path_l has_any (writable_paths)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| extend ExeDir = tolower(strcat(InitiatingProcessFolderPath, "\\"))
| extend DllDir = tolower(strcat(FolderPath, "\\"))
| where ExeDir == DllDir
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceFileCertificateInfo | project SHA1, DllSigned=IsSigned, DllSigner=Signer, DllTrusted=IsTrusted) on $left.SHA1 == $right.SHA1
| where DllSigned == false or isempty(DllSigned) or DllTrusted == false
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, SideloadedDll=FileName, FolderPath, DllSigner, DllSigned, SHA256
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Trend Micro and Secureworks both document that BRONZE BUTLER's primary persistence/execution chain for Daserf, Datper and xxmm is to drop a benign signed host EXE alongside a malicious DLL into a user-writable directory and abuse search-order. The combination of (a) signer = legitimate AV/Japanese-software vendor, (b) execution from %TEMP%/%APPDATA%/ProgramData, and (c) co-located unsigned DLL is

ucs.title: [LLM] BRONZE BUTLER Daserf/Datper C2 beacon to compromised JP/Asia infrastructure with custom-encoded HTTP
ucs.description: Detects long-lived, low-volume HTTP beacons from non-browser processes with the short, fixed-format URI and custom base64-encoded query strings used by Daserf and Datper, which BRONZE BUTLER routes through compromised Japanese SMB websites — a tradecraft pattern unique to this actor's targeting of Japan.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.http_user_agent) as ua values(Web.dest) as dest dc(Web.url) as url_count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.http_method="GET" AND (Web.url="*/index.php?id=*" OR Web.url="*/wp-content/themes/*.php?*" OR Web.url="*/cgi-bin/*.cgi?*" OR match(Web.url,"\?[a-zA-Z]{1,3}=[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{16,}$")) AND NOT (Web.http_user_agent="*Mozilla/5.0*Chrome*" OR Web.http_user_agent="*Edge*" OR Web.http_user_agent="*Firefox*") by Web.src Web.dest Web.http_user_agent Web.app | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | eval beacon_window=(lastTime-firstTime) | where beacon_window > 3600 AND count >= 10 | join type=inner src [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name!="chrome.exe" Processes.process_name!="msedge.exe" Processes.process_name!="firefox.exe" Processes.process_name!="iexplore.exe" by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as src, process_name as initiating_process] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let known_browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","safari.exe"]);
let jp_asia_tlds = dynamic([".jp",".kr",".tw",".cn",".hk"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (known_browsers)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName endswith ".exe"
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\" and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("svchost.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe"))
| summarize hits=count(), firstSeen=min(Timestamp), lastSeen=max(Timestamp), urls=make_set(RemoteUrl, 50), dests=make_set(RemoteIP, 20) by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256, RemoteUrl
| extend beacon_minutes = datetime_diff('minute', lastSeen, firstSeen)
| where hits >= 8 and beacon_minutes >= 60
| where RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\?[a-z]{1,3}=[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{16,}$"
   or RemoteUrl has_any ("/index.php?id=", "/cgi-bin/", "/wp-content/themes/")
| extend jp_target = iff(tolower(RemoteUrl) has_any (jp_asia_tlds), true, false)
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | project DeviceName, ProcessSHA256=SHA256, ProcessCreated=Timestamp, ProcessFolder=FolderPath, ProcessParent=InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on $left.InitiatingProcessSHA256 == $right.ProcessSHA256, DeviceName
| project firstSeen, lastSeen, beacon_minutes, hits, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, ProcessParent, RemoteUrl, urls, dests, jp_target, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| sort by hits desc
ucs.rationale: Secureworks CTU and Trend Micro reporting on Daserf/Datper describe a fingerprint-able C2 channel: a non-browser process beacons over plain HTTP/HTTPS with short URIs ending in `?id=<base64>`, often to legitimate-looking but compromised Japanese SMB CMS sites used as dead-drop resolvers. The query pivots on (a) non-browser EXE, (b) regex-matched custom-encoded query, (c) repetitive low-volume cade
